title: Mindium nudum
taxonomy: Mindium nudum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. This plant is native to Australia and is often found in the eastern regions of the country. It is typically classified as a shrub or small tree, with a height range of around 1-4 meters. The specific classification of Mindium nudum is further detailed within the family Rubiaceae, where it is placed within the genus Mindium. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the specific taxonomic system used.
